metadata.title: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Experimental study with stable isotope technique and hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
metadata.demographics: Ten male and ten female subjects, age 18-35 years, Caucasian race, BMI 20-25 kg/m², no excessive sports activities, no medication, women studied during follicular phase of menstrual cycle

evidence.quote: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
TheJournalofClinicalEndocrinology&Metabolism92(9):3646–3652
Maarten R. Soeters, Hans P. Sauerwein, Johanna E. Groener, Johannes M. Aerts, Mariëtte T. Ackermans, Jan F. C. Glatz, Eric Fliers, and Mireille J. Serlie
Aftera38-hfast,measurementsofglucoseandlipidfluxesandmuscleceramideandfattyacidtranslocase/CD36wereperformedbeforeandafterahyperinsulinemiceuglycemicclamp.

Tenmaleand10femalesubjectswererecruitedviaadvertisementsinlocalmagazines. Criteriaforinclusionwere 1) absenceofafamilyhistoryofdiabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr; 3) Caucasianrace; 4) bodymassindex(BMI)20–25kg/m2; 5) noexcessivesportactivities,i.e.fewerthanhreetimesperweek;and6)nomedication.Womenwerestu diedduringthefollicularphaseofthemenstrualcycle.
Ten male and 10 female subjects were recruited via advertisements in local magazines.
Criteria for inclusion were 1) absence of a family history of diabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr
4) body mass index (BMI) 20–25 kg/m2
the subjects were fasting from 2000h 2d before the start of the study until the end of the study

methods_participants.label: Pre-fasting diet
methods_participants.value: weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250g carbohydrates per day for 3 days

evidence.quote: For 3d before the fasting period, all volunteers consumed a weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250g carbohydrates per day.

methods_participants.label: Glucose measurement timing
methods_participants.value: final 20 minutes of basal state and hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

evidence.quote: Plasma glucose levels were measured every 5 min at the bedside.
infusions of insulin (60mU/m2·min) (Actrapid 100 IU/ml; NovoNordisk FarmaB.V., Alphen aan den Rijn, The Netherlands)
[6,6-2H]glucose (prime, 8.8 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)

Plasma FFA concentrations were determined with an enzymatic colorimetric method (NEFA-C test kit; Wako Chemicals GmbH, Neuss, Germany) with intraassay variation of 1%, interassay variation of 4–15%, and detection limit of 0.02 mmol/liter.

methods_participants.label: Statistical analysis method
methods_participants.value: Mann-Whitney U test for between-group comparisons; Wilcoxon signed rank test for within-group comparisons

evidence.quote: All data were analyzed with nonparametric tests. Comparisons between groups (at T−2 and 8 h) were performed using the Mann-Whitney U test. Comparisons within groups (between T−2 and 8 h) were performed with the Wilcoxon signed rank test.

methods_participants.label: Ceramide and glucosylceramide measurement method
methods_participants.value: 50 μl muscle homogenate used per run

evidence.quote: Muscle biopsies were weighed and homogenized in 300 μl water by sonification, and 50 μl muscle homogenates was used.
Body composition was measured with bioelectrical impedance analysis (Maltron BF906; Maltron, Rayleigh, UK).

methods_participants.label: Muscle biopsy needle type
methods_participants.value: end of basal state and end of hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
methods_participants.method: Pro-Mag I biopsy needle under local anesthesia (lidocaine 20mg/ml)

evidence.quote: The muscle biopsy was performed under local anesthesia (lidocaine 20 mg/ml; Fresenius Kabi, Den Bosch, The Netherlands) using a Pro-Mag I biopsy needle (MDTECH, Gainesville, FL).

All samples were diluted to the same protein concentration and mixed with sample buffer (4:1, vol/vol) before being subjected to SDS-PAGE and immunoblotting exactly as described before (34). The CD36 antigen-antibody band at 88 kDa was visualized with a chemiluminescence substrate (ECL; Amersham Biosciences UK Ltd., Buckinghamshire, UK)

narrative_results.statement: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

evidence.quote: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes

evidence.quote: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes
Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake during the clamp (Rd) did not differ significantly between men and women

narrative_results.statement: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp

evidence.quote: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp
There were no differences in muscle FAT/CD36 content between women and men during both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

evidence.quote: We suggest that during fasting, women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 describes muscle biopsy timing (end of basal state and clamp), while Pass 2 describes biopsy needle type. These are different aspects of the procedure and cannot both be correct for the same field.

structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Adiponectin was determined by a RIA (Linco) with intraassay variation of 2–7%, interassay variation of 16–17%, and detection limit of 1 ng/ml.

semantic_groups.rationale: Identical age inclusion criteria with same units and values
Identical BMI inclusion criteria with same units and values
Identical fasting duration specification with negligible wording variation
Same fact with minor terminology difference: 'Pre-fasting diet' vs 'Pre-study diet' - both describe the same dietary requirement before the study
Identical glucose measurement timing specification
Same blood sampling protocol with minor descriptor difference: pass_1 emphasizes intervals, pass_2 emphasizes method (venipuncture). Both describe identical sampling at T-8h
Same insulin infusion rate with minor terminology difference: 'infusion rate' vs 'infusion dose' - both refer to the same 60 mU/m²·min specification
Pass_1 contains glucose infusion target information with no corresponding entry in pass_2
Both describe the same glucose tracer prime dose (8.8 μmol/kg). Only difference is 'prime dose' vs 'dosing - prime', which are equivalent phrasings.
Both describe the same glucose tracer continuous infusion rate (0.11 μmol/kg·min). Difference is 'continuous infusion rate' vs 'dosing - continuous', which are equivalent.
Both describe the same glycerol tracer prime dose (1.6 μmol/kg). Only difference is 'prime dose' vs 'dosing - prime', which are equivalent phrasings.
Both describe the same glycerol tracer continuous infusion rate (0.11 μmol/kg·min). Difference is 'continuous infusion rate' vs 'dosing - continuous', which are equivalent.
Pass 1 contains equilibration period information (2 hours) but Pass 2 is empty. This is an orphan entry from Pass 1.
Both describe indirect calorimetry timing during final 20 minutes. Pass 2 explicitly mentions 'hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp' while Pass 1 says 'clamp', but this is the same measurement context.

Pass 1 describes muscle biopsy timing (end of basal state and clamp), while Pass 2 describes biopsy needle type. These are different aspects of the procedure and cannot both be correct for the same field.
Both describe glucose measurement using glucose oxidase method with Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2. Pass 2 adds 'Plasma' specification but describes the same measurement method.
Both describe FFA measurement using enzymatic colorimetric method with NEFA-C test kit. Pass 1 specifies manufacturer (Wako Chemicals GmbH), pass 2 omits it, but the core fact is identical.
Both describe insulin measurement using chemiluminescent immunometric assay on Immulite 2000 system. Pass 1 includes manufacturer (Diagnostic Products Corp.), pass 2 omits it, but facts are the same.
Both describe cortisol measurement using chemiluminescent immunoassay on Immulite 2000 system. Pass 1 includes manufacturer details, pass 2 omits them, but core facts match.

Both describe glucagon measurement using 125I RIA from Linco. Pass 1 specifies 'Linco 125I RIA' and adds 'radioimmunoassay (Linco Research)', pass 2 uses 'Linco 125I RIA'. Same underlying fact with minor wording variation.
Both describe ceramide and glucosylceramide measurement using HPLC. Pass 2 adds context that this is from muscle biopsy assay, but the measurement method fact is identical.
Both extractions are identical: women in follicular phase of menstrual cycle.
Both describe saline infusion at 50 ml/h of NaCl 0.9%. Pass 1 includes additional detail about keeping catheters patent, pass 2 omits this. Core fact is the same.
Pass 1 contains statistical analysis method information; pass 2 is empty. This is an orphan entry with no corresponding extraction in pass 2.
Both extractions report identical participant demographics: 10 male and 10 female subjects
Pass 1 has no content while Pass 2 contains muscle biopsy sample preparation details. This is an orphan entry present only in Pass 2
Pass 1 has no content while Pass 2 contains body composition measurement method details. This is an orphan entry present only in Pass 2
Pass 1 has no content while Pass 2 contains adiponectin measurement assay details. This is an orphan entry present only in Pass 2
Pass 1 has no content while Pass 2 contains FAT/CD36 measurement method details. This is an orphan entry present only in Pass 2
Identical statements about basal plasma FFA levels in women vs men
Identical statements about insulin-mediated glucose uptake between sexes
Identical statements about basal muscle ceramide gender differences
Identical statements about ceramide-glucose uptake correlation
Identical statements about muscle CD36 levels across conditions and sexes
Identical statements about adiponectin levels in females across conditions
Identical statements about plasma epinephrine levels during clamp
Identical statements about female protection from FFA-induced insulin resistance via ceramide prevention
Identical statements about plasma glucose levels being significantly lower in women than men
Pass 1 is empty; only Pass 2 contains information about lipolysis rates in women
